title: Void Value
documentation:
## Purpose
The [Void Value](https://capdag.com/browse/media:void) media type represents the absence of a value — analogous to `void` in programming languages or `null` in type theory. It is used to type ports or outputs that carry no data payload.

## When to use
Use this type for node outputs that signal completion or success without returning any data — for example, a side-effectful node that writes to a database and has nothing to return, or a terminal sink node. It is also appropriate as the type of an optional input port whose absence is meaningful.

## Notes
A void value produces no bytes; its byte length is always zero. Implementers should not transmit any payload when producing a void value, and consumers should ignore any payload bytes if present. This type is intentionally distinct from an empty byte sequence (`media:`) to make the semantic "no output" explicit in the graph schema.
